Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. More precise elevation data for GraphHopper routing engine 59 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. Two studies in compiler optimisations 88 upvotes, 11 comments
  3. Quantization from the Ground Up 277 upvotes, 50 comments
  4. The Last Contract: William T. Vollmann's Battle to Publish an Epic (2025) 15 upvotes, 0 comments
  5. Personal Encyclopedias 293 upvotes, 63 comments
  6. Obsolete Sounds 53 upvotes, 7 comments
  7. The EU still wants to scan your private messages and photos 1253 upvotes, 335 comments
  8. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 663 upvotes, 217 comments
  9. My DIY FPGA board can run Quake II 166 upvotes, 51 comments
  10. Thoughts on slowing the fuck down 898 upvotes, 398 comments
  11. False claims in a widely-cited paper 298 upvotes, 122 comments
  12. From zero to a RAG system: successes and failures 36 upvotes, 10 comments
  13. Swift 6.3 109 upvotes, 46 comments
  14. Shell Tricks That Make Life Easier (and Save Your Sanity) 151 upvotes, 68 comments
  15. Government agencies buy commercial data about Americans in bulk 82 upvotes, 33 comments
  16. The Cassandra of 'The Machine' 16 upvotes, 3 comments
  17. Earthquake scientists reveal how overplowing weakens soil at experimental farm 172 upvotes, 88 comments
  18. LibreOffice and the Art of Overreacting 43 upvotes, 17 comments
  19. ARC-AGI-3 419 upvotes, 264 comments
  20. 90% of Claude-linked output going to GitHub repos w <2 stars 309 upvotes, 190 comments
  21. Optimization lessons from a Minecraft structure locator 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  22. The truth that haunts the Ramones: 'They sold more T-shirts than records' 154 upvotes, 93 comments
  23. Show HN: Optio – Orchestrate AI coding agents in K8s to go from ticket to PR 59 upvotes, 33 comments
  24. Supreme Court Sides with Cox in Copyright Fight over Pirated Music 361 upvotes, 281 comments
  25. Maxell MXCP-P100 – wireless cassette player 34 upvotes, 18 comments
  26. "Disregard That" Attacks 92 upvotes, 64 comments
  27. Niche Museums 18 upvotes, 8 comments
  28. Show HN: Robust LLM Extractor for Websites in TypeScript 48 upvotes, 34 comments
  29. What came after the 486? 53 upvotes, 46 comments
  30. Ashby (YC W19) Is Hiring Engineers Who Make Product Decisions 0 upvotes, 0 comments
